title: Knee Injury in Athlete - ACL Tear
chief_complaint: 22-year-old football player twisted his right knee during match. Heard a 'pop', immediate swelling within 2 hours, and knee gave way. Now feels unstable.

physical_exam.knee: Large effusion (hemarthrosis), positive Lachman test, positive anterior drawer, positive pivot shift, tenderness over lateral joint line

hidden_data.mri_knee: Complete tear of ACL, bone contusions lateral femoral condyle and lateral tibial plateau, lateral meniscus tear

correct_diagnosis: Complete ACL Tear with Associated Lateral Meniscus Tear
key_learning_points: Pop + immediate swelling = ACL tear until proven
MRI confirms and evaluates associated injuries
ACL reconstruction for young active patients

patient_responses.mechanism: I planted my foot and twisted to change direction
patient_responses.pop: I heard a loud pop and felt my knee shift
patient_responses.swelling: It swelled up within an hour

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! ACL tear with classic mechanism and Lachman.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is an ACL tear. The pop + hemarthrosis + positive Lachman are classic.

mri_knee.comment: Confirms ACL tear and associated injuries.

feedback.attending_note: ACL tear: Pre-hab with PT for ROM and quad strengthening. ACL reconstruction with hamstring or patellar tendon autograft. Return to sport 9-12 months post-op. Concurrent meniscus repair if indicated.
